name: Hash Map + Index Boundaries
summary: Pre-index every inorder value; take the next preorder value as each subtree's root and split its inorder range at that root in O(1) — no searching, no slicing.
visualizationIntent: init: the stage starts empty; the preorder and inorder arrays appear as aux strips and an inorder-index map is built. construct: the pre_idx pointer takes the next preorder value as the current subtree's root — a new tree node pops in and links to its parent (left or right); the root's position in inorder (middle) splits the active [left..right] window into a left range and a right range. recurse: descend into the left window (left..middle-1) then the right window (middle+1..right), so the tree grows top-down in preorder order while the inorder window narrows at each level. done: the finished tree is serialized to level-order as the result.

lineExplanations.3: Count how many nodes the tree will have.
lineExplanations.4: Start an empty list of node objects for the drawing.
lineExplanations.5: Start a lookup from a node's id to its object.
lineExplanations.6: Start a per-node state tracker: unseen, building, or done.
lineExplanations.7: Loop over every future node to initialize its state.
lineExplanations.9: Start a map from each value to its index in inorder.
lineExplanations.10: Loop over the inorder array to fill that map.
lineExplanations.11: Record that value inorder[i] sits at index i.
lineExplanations.12: A one-slot holder for the next preorder position (starts at 0).
lineExplanations.13: Build the whole tree from the full inorder range; get the root id.
lineExplanations.14: Serialize the finished tree to level-order form.
lineExplanations.18: Base case: the range is empty — no node here.
lineExplanations.19: Return None for an empty range.
lineExplanations.20: The next preorder value becomes this subtree's root id.
lineExplanations.21: Read that root's value from preorder.
lineExplanations.22: Advance the preorder pointer past this root.
lineExplanations.23: Create the root node with empty child links.
lineExplanations.24: Add the new node to the tree.
lineExplanations.25: Register it under its id for fast lookup.
lineExplanations.26: Mark it building (on the call stack).
lineExplanations.27: Does this node have a parent to attach to?
lineExplanations.28: Is it the parent's left child?
lineExplanations.29: Attach it as the parent's left child.
lineExplanations.31: Otherwise attach it as the parent's right child.
lineExplanations.32: Find the root's index in inorder — it splits left from right.
lineExplanations.33: Build the left subtree from the inorder range before the root.
lineExplanations.34: Build the right subtree from the inorder range after the root.
lineExplanations.35: Both subtrees built — mark this node done.
lineExplanations.36: Return this node's id to its parent.
lineExplanations.40: Start a queue to walk the tree breadth-first.
lineExplanations.41: Seed the walk with the root if the tree is non-empty.
lineExplanations.44: Take the next id from the front of the queue.
lineExplanations.45: A None entry marks a missing child.
lineExplanations.46: Emit null for that missing child.
lineExplanations.51: Trim trailing nulls the level-order format omits.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def buildTree(self, preorder, inorder): the entry method; the two traversal arrays.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(preorder) is the number of nodes.
syntaxExplanations.5: by_id = {} creates an empty dict (id -> node).
syntaxExplanations.6: state_by_id = [] will hold one status per node id.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(n): loops i from 0 to n-1.
syntaxExplanations.8: append(0) adds an unseen marker for this slot.
syntaxExplanations.9: inorder_index = {} creates an empty dict (value -> index).
syntaxExplanations.10: for i in range(n): loop over inorder positions.
syntaxExplanations.11: inorder_index[inorder[i]] = i stores each value's index.
syntaxExplanations.12: pre_idx = [0] is a one-element list used as a mutable pointer.
syntaxExplanations.13: self.construct(...) builds the tree and returns the root id.
syntaxExplanations.14: self.serialize(...) turns the tree into a level-order list.
syntaxExplanations.15: return result hands back the answer.
syntaxExplanations.17: def construct(self, preorder, left, right, ...): builds the subtree whose inorder range is [left, right].
syntaxExplanations.18: if left > right: an empty range.
syntaxExplanations.19: return None: no node for an empty range.
syntaxExplanations.20: node_id = pre_idx[0] reads the next preorder position.
syntaxExplanations.21: preorder[node_id] is this subtree's root value.
syntaxExplanations.23: A dict literal builds the node object.
syntaxExplanations.24: nodes.append(node) adds it to the drawing.
syntaxExplanations.27: if parent_id != -1: skip linking for the overall root.
syntaxExplanations.28: if side == 0: it is a left child.
syntaxExplanations.30: else: it is a right child.
syntaxExplanations.32: inorder_index[root_value] is the root's inorder index (the split point).
syntaxExplanations.33: self.construct(..., left, middle-1, ...) builds the left subtree.
syntaxExplanations.34: self.construct(..., middle+1, right, ...) builds the right subtree.
syntaxExplanations.36: return node_id gives the parent this subtree's root.
syntaxExplanations.38: def serialize(self, root, by_id): BFS the tree into a level-order list.
syntaxExplanations.40: out_q = [] is a list used as a queue.
syntaxExplanations.41: if root is not None: only walk a non-empty tree.
syntaxExplanations.43: while len(out_q) > 0: until the queue empties.
syntaxExplanations.45: if emit_id is None: a missing child.
syntaxExplanations.51: while ... is None: trim trailing nulls.
syntaxExplanations.53: return result hands back the level-order array.
